entry.title: Re: Replenishment component not displayed in checkout – C...

entry.title: Re: Is there any tool like SAP DART for public cloud?
entry.content:
Look at SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for

  • Scope item 5XU (SAP Document and Reporting Compliance)
  • Scope item 78L (SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition Integration
  • 3387894 - Integration of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Release information about electronic documents and reports

entry.title: Re: Need to find whcih version of SAP BO supports RHEL 10
entry.content: Hello All. PAM is scheduled to be updated by end of this week. please check back for updates around platform support.

entry.title: New Here - Product Manager for SAP EarlyWatch Alert
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

my name is Esther,  and I’m excited to join the SAP Community as a Product Manager for SAP EarlyWatch Alert.

I started in this role last year, helping shape the product roadmap, collaborating with customers and partners, and ensuring we deliver valuable features that meet real business needs. Being part of this community is something I truly value, because knowledge grows through collaboration.

Why I’m here

My goal in this community is simple:

  • Share knowledge about SAP EarlyWatch Alert—from basics to advanced topics
  • Provide first-hand insights into features, updates
  • Offer practical guidance based on real-world use cases

What you can expect from me

I plan to contribute with:

  • Tips and best practices
  • Explanations of new features and capabilities
  • Guidance on how to get the most out of SAP EarlyWatch Alert.

You may have already seen posts on these topics from user SusanneGlaenzer — I’d like to build on that and continue the conversation.

I’m looking forward to engaging with all of you and learning together!

Thanks for having me here,
Esther

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Connector, Tomcat related question
entry.content:
I am surprised that you did not get an answer to this question in a SAP support ticket. From my point of view, I don't see a reason why not?! Can you tell me the support ticket number?
But in a public forum like here, anyone can create an account. I don't know if you are an SAP customer, or a social engineer trying to gather some info for potential malicious attacks. I am not telling that you are, but that's the reason. It's a basic security principle not to disclose any information about the software, libraries and versions being used to arbitrary/anonymous users (need-to-know principle).

Regarding the relevance for an upgrade, the answer is that considering the security aspect, one should always upgrade Cloud Connector to its latest patch level. And besides, it is not supported that Cloud Connector's bundled Tomcat libraries are upgraded alone on your own. However, every new Cloud Connector version always comes with the latest available libraries that successfully passed all tests shortly before each release.

entry.title: Workflow not triggering
entry.content: i created this rule, when selecting the event and event reason as promotion.... and when saving the record in employee profile, its not triggering the workflow WF_EC_PROMOTION.

entry.title: Re: GL Role Restriction based on Company Code.
entry.content:
Hi  @ksiddiqui , 

As we can see on your screenshots you are trying to set restrictions to company code, however, the visibility is controlled by the restriction types labeled Read, Value Help / or only Read. Only the Write access is restricted on the "General" tab.

 

To do that you should set the restrictions on the access category to "restricted" to at least (write, read, value help) and (read, value help). 

Than on company code, you select the company codes you want to RESTRICT access.

 

On this demonstration case below (only for illustration), I set the restriction to company code 1010, so the user won't be able to see and write on this company code.

You can also can see that I've used the Leading Restrictions check box:

"That means that the value in this field is automatically inherited by other restriction types that also use that field."

 

You can find some more info here: How to Define Authorizations Based on Restrictions | SAP Help Portal

Also, you can check more detailed step by step info on the unit 5 of this Learning Path:

Performing Advanced Configuration and Role Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, EHS Envi…

Another tip: To understand which restrictions are granting access to certain values you can use the App Display Authorization Trace, detailed on unit 6 as well.

I hope this helps,

Kind Regards,

Raffaella

entry.title: SAP SFX Administration Masterclasses
entry.content:

Do you guys know what happened to the SAP SFX Administration Masterclasses that were offered to customers?

They were always posted here, however there have not been any updates since last year.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Connector, Tomcat related question
entry.content: I think I might have phrased it wrong regarding the customer support part. I went to the site, pressed on contact us and filled out the information. I then received and email from: 'sap@mail.sap.com' , pretty much telling me for technical questions to either go through the SAP Customer channels. Which I cannot, as my team aren't personally customers. The other option talked about in the email was to come here and ask. Like I said, now I have my answer. If I want to know the version, I either a:) download it and check or b:) ask the client to go through the SAP Customer channels. Thanks for you help, I really appreciate it!

entry.title: Re: Credit management tolerance not working for Service o...
entry.content:
Hi,

You should be able to find the following path depending on your system version:

Financial Supply Chain Management -> Credit Management -> Integration with Accounts Receivable Accounting and Sales and Distribution -> Integration with Service

You could refer to the following Help Page links for Credit Management for Service contracts and orders:

 

Credit Management for Service Contracts

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c9b5e9de6e674fb99fff88d72c352291/2f7ce9927b424492b5e3e25656e8bfca.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

Credit Management for Service Orders

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c9b5e9de6e674fb99fff88d72c352291/830695f64e6a4c31b8a9f121c3cd8733.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST&state=PRODUCTION

I suppose the tolerance you mean is the deviation after the sales order is released. This post is tagged as SD sales topic and that setting is meant for the SD document configurations. We do not find a similar one for the Service documents.

Maybe someone from CRM-S4-BF-CM could give you some workaround. 

Best regards,
Rodrigo

entry.title: Lemur maps integration with SSAM
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have Lemur maps integrated with SSAM 2310, would like to know when a user raises a Notification from map - where can we find the data(FL/EQ/customer data/geometries..etc) passed from maps to SSAM. I have tried to check in SSAM logs but I could not any relevant data.

Thank you.

SAP Service and Asset Manager 

Regards,

Sravanthi

entry.title: Fiori Elements ALP (OData V2): Basic Search field shown despite @Search.searchable: false in CDS
entry.content:
Problem:

In an Analytical List Page (ALP) application based on a directly exposed CDS view, the Basic Search field is displayed in the SmartFilterBar, even though search is explicitly disabled by using the CDS Annotation: @search.searchable: false.

Analytical List Page (OData V2, UI5 1.71, SAP_BASIS 750)

Expectation: Basic Search should be hidden.
Actual behavior: Basic Search is still displayed.

Important:

  • No @search.* annotations on field level
  • Only @search.searchable: false on view level

Findings
The behavior appears to contradict the expected semantics of the annotation.
In this setup (SAP_BASIS 750 + UI5 1.71 + OData V2), the system behaves as follows:

The mere presence of the annotation @search.searchable (regardless of its value) seems to activate search capabilities in the OData service and UI.

As a result:

The SmartFilterBar interprets the service as search-enabled
The Basic Search field is displayed, even when set to false

Workaround
Remove (or comment out) the annotation completely
@search.searchable: false

Side Effect

After removing the annotation, the CDS view produces a syntax/activation warning or error similar to:
"@Search annotations require 'searchable' for the view"

However:

If the CDS is still activated (ignoring the warning)
The resulting behavior is that the Basic Search field is no longer displayed

entry.title: Re: Conversion to S/4HANA: Issue with custom long texts and concatenated TDNAME including MATNR
entry.content:
I believe the text 'below' pertains to 'Customer Extensions and Custom Customer Tables'.

During the migration process, this data is neither considered nor converted. If the customer utilizes extension fields and/or custom tables with concatenated fields, they must manage this data independently. Please note that pure material number fields do not require conversion.

You could start from 2216958 - Precheck Program for Migration to SAP S/4HANA with regards to Material Number Field Length Extension: Find BOR Usage and Material Number Usage but expect a 'If a conversion is necessary, it needs to be done by the customer.'

entry.title: Required to Split Debit Entry in Sales Invoice Journal Entry
entry.content:
Hello team,

We require assistance regarding journal entry posting behavior for Sales Invoices in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Current System Behavior:
When a sales invoice is posted, the system creates:

  • One combined debit line to the Customer account containing both item value and tax amount
  • Separate credit lines for revenue accounts/profit centers

Expected Requirement:
We would like the debit side to also be split into separate lines, for example:

  • Debit Line 1 → Item/Net Amount
  • Debit Line 2 → Tax Amount

This is required for better reporting, reconciliation, and visibility of tax vs. item values in journal entries.

We would like SAP’s guidance on:

  1. Whether this functionality is supported in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud standard
  2. If any SSCUI/configuration is available to enable this behavior
  3. Whether this can be achieved through configuration of document splitting, accounting determination, or another standard feature
  4. If not available in standard Public Cloud, whether any extensibility option (BAdI/In-App Extensibility) exists

Please refer to the attached screenshot as an example of the desired split structure.

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Pros and Cons of implementing full MDG within Central finance in sap
entry.content:
Hi MDG Experts,

We are implementing Central finance now and also want to implement SAP MDG for the data governance later.

We have options to host MDG on a separate Hub system or to host MDG within Central finance (embedded/co-deployed MDG).

Experts, can you please throw light on the pros and cons of implementing MDG within Central finance and any considerations compare to MDG Hub approach?

Thanks

entry.title: Embracing AI for planning and analysis in SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
AI is moving fast.  Partners are asking sharper questions, customers expect smarter answers, and the pressure to turn data into action keeps growing.

That’s exactly why I’m excited about this upcoming session: “Embracing AI for planning and analysis in SAP Business Data Cloud.”

We’ll explore how AI is shaping analytics and planning across SAP Business Data Cloud, including innovations like Joule, Just Ask, and AI-Assisted capabilities available today, plus a look into the future roadmap.

The thing is, AI conversations can sometimes feel abstract.  This session keeps it practical.  Think faster insights, less manual effort, and better planning decisions across finance and operations.

Plus, we’ll leave time for live Q&A, because the best conversations usually happen when partners bring real-world scenarios to the table.

If your customers are asking how AI fits into planning and analytics, this webinar will give you useful talking points and examples you can apply right away.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/jul/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010880.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPBusinessDataCloud #BusinessAI #Analytics #Planning

entry.title: Re: Fiori List Report Issue with OData V4 VS V2
entry.content: Hi Team, This is happening when the Backend CDS is Parameterized CDS view Entity. If the CDS is normal then the application is working as expected. As the Parameterized CDS is having an Mandatory as input filters. Remaining filters are also in the UI marked as Mandatory but without passing also GO button is working. Thanks Venkat

entry.title: SAP EarlyWatch Alert: UI Refresh for Improved Readability and Usability
entry.content:
We’re happy to share a refreshed user interface for the SAP EarlyWatch Alert. This is the first milestone in our ongoing journey to make end users and organizations work simpler, faster, and more efficiently.

 

 

Refreshed user interface for SAP EarlyWatch Alert

The changes focus exclusively on visual improvements, there are no modifications to functionality, navigation, or available content. All existing apps, workflows, and entry points remain the same.

What Has Changed?

The UI refresh improves how SAP EarlyWatch Alert content is presented:

  • Cleaner layouts and improved spacing
  • Consistent typography and visual structure
  • Clearer highlighting of important findings and alerts
  • Improved readability of reports and tables

The goal is to make the rich SAP EarlyWatch Alert insights easier to scan, understand, and consume—especially during daily checks or focused analysis.

What Has Not Changed?

This refresh does not introduce:

  • New applications or features
  • Changes to navigation or workflows
  • New KPIs, checks, or alert logic

Everything you know and use today remains in place.

Why a UI Refresh Matters?           

SAP EarlyWatch Alert provides weekly insights to manage SAP system health. It contains technical information that helps teams, enabling predictive and preventive actions to mitigate risks. While the content itself remains unchanged, a clearer and more consistent visual design reduces cognitive effort and helps users:

  • Find relevant information faster
  • Read and interpret findings more efficiently
  • Use SAP EarlyWatch Alert more comfortably

This is a low‑risk, high‑impact improvement that enhances usability without disrupting established processes.

One-Time Adjustments When Accessing the New UI

Migration of Existing Views on First Access

When users access the SAP EarlyWatch Alert Workspace in the new UI for the first time, any previously created and saved views are automatically migrated to the new user interface. This is a one-time process designed to ensure continuity of existing working setups without requiring manual action.

During this step, a short message informs users that the environment is being prepared and that saved views are being migrated in the background. Once the migration is completed successfully, users can reload the workspace and continue working with their existing views as usual.

No additional steps are required. The migration is handled automatically and does not affect the content or logic of the views.

Renaming of Applications

As part of the UI update, some SAP EarlyWatch Alert applications have been renamed to better reflect their purpose and terminology. The underlying functionality of these applications remains unchanged.

The following name changes apply:

  • SAP HANA Table Records (previously 2 Billion Record Limit)
  • Number Ranges (previously Critical Number Ranges)
  • SAP HANA Indexserver: Memory Usage (previously Main Memory SAP HANA)

These changes are limited to naming and presentation only and do not impact the content or behavior of the applications.

Summary

By aligning SAP EarlyWatch Alert with the consistent look and feel of SAP for Me, we ensure a more coherent digital experience across SAP touchpoints — laying the groundwork for future innovations without disrupting existing usage today.

The refreshed SAP EarlyWatch Alert UI keeps the tool exactly as you know it—just clearer, more consistent, and easier to read. Sometimes, improving the familiar is already a meaningful step forward.

 Direct Access Links

For users who want to access specific SAP EarlyWatch Alert applications directly, the following links are available in SAP for Me:

  • SAP EarlyWatch Alert Workspace  https://me.sap.com/ewa 
  • SAP EarlyWatch Alert Reports https://me.sap.com/ewa/reports
  • SAP EarlyWatch Alert Dashboard https://me.sap.com/ewa/dashboard
  • SAP EarlyWatch Alert Solution Finder https://me.sap.com/ewa/solutionfinder 
  • SAP EarlyWatch Alert Status https://me.sap.com/ewa/status
  • SAP HANA Table Records https://me.sap.com/ewa/tablerecordsforecast
  • Number Ranges https://me.sap.com/ewa/numberrangeforecast
  • SAP HANA Indexserver: Memory Usage https://me.sap.com/ewa/indexmemoryforecast

Access permissions and availability depend on the customer’s system landscape and authorizations.

 Further Information

  • Landing Page: SAP EarlyWatch Alert in SAP Support

Other blogs you might be interested in

  • 🎉Celebrating the Successful Launch of Joule in SAP for Me

  • Video Joule in SAP for Me - SAP’s Unified, AI‑Powered Gateway to SAP for Me

entry.title: Why SAP Document AI Matters More Than Most Enterprises Think
entry.content:
Introduction

When everyone gets excited about the same topics, conversations will naturally center around them. Eventually, that excitement will catch on, and you’ll find yourself on the same wavelength as everyone else. Currently, as businesses focus on enterprise AI projects, most discussions revolve around similar themes: copilot, agents, and large language models. However, despite significant investments in AI, many businesses still face a common operational obstacle: documents.

Regardless of the industry, invoices, purchase orders, supplier forms, shipping documents, contracts, and compliance documents flow between departments daily. Each requires some degree of manual verification or validation and often involves multiple systems or workflows. This is where SAP Document AI comes in.

SAP Document AI transforms unstructured business documents into structured operational data, helping businesses modernize their operations. Instead of just digitizing your documents, you can use Document AI to make them useful and functional within your business. Even with advancements in intelligent front-end experiences, the foundational infrastructure supporting these solutions still relies on manual verification, various data extraction methods, and disconnected workflows. Document intelligence within SAP environments has matured, and companies now view it as a key automation initiative rather than a secondary option.

Our Experience

Adopting Document AI can reduce the need for labor to support business functions, leading to more efficient solutions. SAP Document AI addresses a common challenge in enterprise environments: extracting, understanding, and organizing information from business documents. In many companies, important operational data arrives in formats like PDFs, scanned files, emails, invoices, and forms, which ERP systems struggle to process without manual help.

SAP Document AI lowers this friction by using AI to identify document types, extract relevant fields, classify information, and prepare that data for business workflows. This means organizations can automate many document-heavy processes across finance, procurement, supply chain, HR, and other operational areas, rather than having employees manually review documents and transfer information between systems. The value of using Document AI goes beyond faster document processing; it empowers operational workflows to operate with greater speed, consistency, and scalability. As enterprises manage more operational data, minimizing manual document reliance becomes essential for maintaining efficiency.

We had the opportunity to see this first-hand. In this scenario, the company was operating in the oil and gas industry, where customer representatives and field engineers constantly generate operational documents while working across remote locations. Every service ticket, carrier document, export filing, client approval, invoice, or credit memo needed to be attached to the correct Sales Order inside SAP for compliance and operational traceability purposes. The challenge was that these documents arrived through emails in different languages, layouts, and formats, often with little context beyond a subject line. As a result, employees had to manually open every file, identify what type of document it was, locate the Sales Order reference somewhere inside the content, rename the file according to internal naming conventions, and upload it into SAP. At a scale of more than 8,000 documents per month, this translated into over 1,000 hours of manual effort every month, distributed across teams in multiple regions.

The organization already had partial automation in place through legacy capture tools and batch jobs, but the process still depended heavily on human intervention before the automation could even begin. A single typo in a file name or an incorrectly classified document could silently break the workflow and prevent the document from being linked correctly inside SAP. By implementing SAP Document AI on SAP BTP, the process shifted from manual interpretation to intelligent document processing. Incoming documents were automatically picked up through SAP Integration Suite, classified using SAP Document AI, and processed to extract key operational data such as Sales Order numbers, vendor details, amounts, and document dates. The extracted information was then validated and routed directly into SAP workflows, significantly reducing manual effort while improving scalability, consistency, and operational visibility across the entire process.

Why This Matters Beyond Document Processing

What made this project valuable was not just the automation itself. I would say it was rather the impact it had on daily operations. Once documents could be processed and validated in a more consistent way, teams spent far less time trying to interpret files manually or fixing small mistakes that slowed everything down. Workflows became easier to manage across different regions, and the process no longer depended so heavily on people knowing specific naming conventions or internal steps that were never formally documented.

Over time, we also realized that SAP Document AI was doing much more than helping with document processing. It became part of the operational structure supporting the entire workflow. As companies continue investing in SAP Business AI and intelligent workflows, having reliable operational data becomes increasingly important. If documents are still disconnected from the systems around them, scaling automation becomes much harder than expected.

Conclusion

As we said at the beginning, a lot of the conversation around enterprise AI focuses on what people can immediately see: copilots, AI agents, chat interfaces, and new user experiences. At the same time, many operational problems still happen behind the scenes, especially in processes where important business information is buried inside emails, PDFs, scanned files, and manual workflows.

Our experience with SAP Document AI showed us that document intelligence is no longer just a secondary automation tool. It can play an important role in helping businesses create cleaner and more scalable operations. When documents become structured operational data instead of isolated files, workflows move faster, teams spend less time on repetitive tasks, and enterprise systems become easier to scale over time.

entry.title: Re: Reusing Data Nodes to Display a Table More Than Once in Adobe Forms
entry.content:
Amazing blog! I was stuck on this for hours until I found this, other solutions were mentioning using context level to add a copy of internal table, but working in SAP Public Cloud that wasn't an option for me. What I had to change is, because I have two different flowed containers in one page I had to change this part:
From: xfa.resolveNodes("GT_MARA.DATA[*]").length; 

To: xfa.resolveNodes("Form.subform.tblTable.rowRow1[*]").length;
I had to specify the Form.subform as subforms where the original table was and I had to specify the row itself as my bindings on the original table where on the row level instead of the table level.

entry.title: Re: Latest People Profile Cards Configuration - Move to a...
entry.content:
Hi @geenaouellette 

We have the same problem with the predefined Trend Information card which (as far as I can see) cannot be moved to another category.
Would love to hear it as well when you have an answer to your first question.

Kind regards,
Yanik

entry.title: Down payment in extended intercompany process 5D2 to the customer and within the company
entry.content:
In SAP Public Cloud, is it possible to define a billing plan in the sales order for the end customer within the extended intercompany process (Scope Item 5D2) that is carried over to the automatically generated internal sales order and can also be adapted there?

If not, what is the best way to handle this if I want to store a billing plan in the sales order to the end customer but also need a down payment from the other company code for internal settlement? Is there a way to handle this through Finance?

entry.title: Re: Conversion to S/4HANA: Issue with custom long texts and concatenated TDNAME including MATNR
entry.content:
Thanks @VXLozano! Yes, I know that SAP changed MATNR from 18 to 40 characters in lenght, but during the conversion we tell the system to only consider 18 characters. 

If we had control over the code where the concatenation of MATNR + VKORG + VTWEG to build TDNAME happens, we could either use 18 characters (for long texts created prior to the conversion) or 40 characters (anything newly created in S/4HANA) but - at least in the MM0n transactions this is happing (deep) within SAP logic, so out of reach for us. 

And we cannot change the data type for the import parameters used in FM LANGTEXT_INIT.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and ECTR
entry.content:
Hi Christian,

Regarding your initial questions:

  • How does SAP envision R&D‑heavy customers adopting S/4HANA Cloud, public edition without ECTR?
    • Well, there is an alternative for ECTR (respective CAD tool integrations) for S/4HANA public cloud. This capability is called Product Data Integration and part of SAP Integrated Product Development (IPD).
    • For "R&D-heavy" we also have extended capabilities like requirements management, product structure management and collaboration within IPD.
  • Are hybrid scenarios a long‑term target architecture or only a transition phase?
    • Hm?!? You can start right away with a pure SaaS offering and do not need a hybrid scenario (on premise installation and SaaS-solutions).
  • Is there a public‑cloud roadmap that could eventually cover ECTR‑like capabilities?
    • Yes, please see here

Regarding your second questions/comments:

  • One clarification question from my side, building on the point that there is currently no intended cloud alternative to ECTR: 
    • Answer: the assumption "no intended cloud alternative to ECTR" is wrong. We plan a SaaS-cloud offering for CAD integration, see Product Data Integration
  • For customers who are nevertheless keen on moving to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, how should SAP Integrated Product Development (IPD) be understood in this context? 
    • Answer: IPD is a full PLM SaaS offering to manage the iterative product development process with a deep integration and handover to S/4HANA public cloud.
  • Specifically: Is IPD meant to address any of the engineering / PLM use cases that ECTR covers today, or is it targeting fundamentally different scenarios? 
    • Answer: IPD replaces a couple of functions know by ECTR, e.g. CAD integration. ECTR is also "antoher" User Interface on top of Material Management, BOM Management, Change Management or Document Management. IPD will provide similar capabilities to support the product development process.
  • Should R&D‑heavy, CAD‑centric customers view IPD as part of a possible cloud‑oriented approach, or should they assume that certain ECTR‑type capabilities simply have no public‑cloud equivalent at this point? Any guidance on how SAP positions IPD relative to ECTR in a public‑cloud target architecture would be very helpful.
    • Answer: see above

 

Hope this helps.

In case you need more information, I'm happy to talk to you.

Regards,

Gerhard Himmelsbach (Linked IN: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gerhard-himmelsbach-4484b6b/ )

- Product Manager for CAD integrations (ECTR, PDI, CAD-Desktop) -

entry.title: Re: Business maintain condition contract BUT do not want...
entry.content: Closing the discussion, as business agreed to go with Settlement via Condition Contact Settlement (WB2R_SV)

entry.title: Re: SAP Crystal Reports libcurl.dll Vulnerabilities
entry.content:
Hi All,

I've worked for SAP for 25 years, retired now but still have access to them, and discussed CVE's with R&D extensively.

There are a lot of scanners out there that find security issues and often they are False/Positives, meaning there are holes but if the application using the references does not access the reported function there is nothing SAP needs to do to resolve those threats within SAP Products.

SAP takes these reported CVE's very seriously and will fix what affects SAP Products only, they won't update just because there are newer versions, unless they are no longer supported.

Also note: SAP has "custom" versions of some resources that specifically have been fixed for known CVE's.

So just because a scanner reports an issue there is nothing they need to do to fix it if SAP Products don't access the reported functions.

See this KBA R&D Provided:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1475602

Note:

Symptom

A third-party tool such as WebInspect or AppScan has been pointed at a deployment of a SAP Business Objects product (BI 4.x/20xx- 4.1, 4.2 , 4.3, 2025 ) and the resulting report shows a number of security vulnerabilities (such as Cross Site Scripting).

  • How are these issues addressed by SAP Support?
  • What are the responsibilities of the customer?
  • What are the responsibilities of SAP engineers?

It goes on to indicate if you can provide an application using Crystal runtime and showing there is an issue R&D will resolve the problem....

Be aware the code stream for Crystal Reports include all of the BOE/CR Server products, so replacing 1 file is a huge amount of testing etc., of course if it's required it will be done.

So I and R&D appreciate your concern but just because some scanner reports an issue doesn't mean it's going to be vulnerable in products that don't access those functions.

Also, there is no path to purchase a support contract for Crystal Reports, only option is to purchase Crystal Reports Server, you get a 1 year support contract and access to the support Portal: https://support.sap.com/en/index.html

To Sum up:

Not all CVE's are valid issues using SAP Crystal/BOE Products

If you have concerns please provide a sample application showing how the specific CVE's can be accessed using CR runtime.

Hope that clears things up?

Don

entry.title: Re: Integrating IRIS
entry.content: Hi, please check this out: https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blog-posts-by-sap/important-update-what-s-changing-in-us-federal-regulatory-tax-reporting-and/ba-p/14389181 . entry.title: Re: Conversion to S/4HANA: Issue with custom long texts and concatenated TDNAME including MATNR
entry.content: Thanks, @Ryan-Crosby! I doubt that a conversion script would work and provide consistent results as TDNAME is a keyfield in at least STXH and STXL but possibly more long-text related tables, based on what I saw in SAT-results. This is why I'd really like to "get my hands on" the conversion program hinted at in OSS Note 2215871 to see how SAP does it for the standard long texts with concatenated TDNAME which include MATNR. What works for those should also work for custom long texts, shouldn't it?

entry.title: Re: debugging software that connects to SAP ASE
entry.content: @ChrisBaker, where do I report it? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Celebrate Your SAP Academic Community Conference experience: Earn Your Exclusive Conference Badg
entry.content: I attended the SAP ACC LAC 2026 and I’m very happy to have spent this Days in Sao Leopoldo SAP labs, sharing experiencies with colleagues from the region, and Learning interesting topics that we can include in our curricula. Thanks a lot for this opportunity.

entry.title: Re: Celebrate Your SAP Academic Community Conference experience: Earn Your Exclusive Conference Badg
entry.content: Gracias por darnos la oportunidad de reunirnos!!

entry.title: Re: Celebrate Your SAP Academic Community Conference experience: Earn Your Exclusive Conference Badg
entry.content:
Gracias por estas 3 excelentes jornadas.

Conocimiento, desafíos por delante y muchas valiosas experiencias compartidas por profesores y expertos de distintas áreas de toda la región.

entry.title: Re: create identity column

entry.title: Re: Celebrate Your SAP Academic Community Conference experience: Earn Your Exclusive Conference Badg
entry.content:
Thank you all for the kind words and support. It was an incredible opportunity to exchange experiences and learn from one another.

A special thank you to Maria Calzada. Your insight and support were invaluable, and it was a pleasure to exchange experiences with you.

entry.title: Re: Important Update: Review Onboarding User Permissions
entry.content:
Hi @Sevda,

I believe that SAP was able to solve this issue within Latest Full Profile. We are live with Full Profile and we have granted mentioned permissions to the end-user roles. There is no unintended exposure of employee data.

Thanks,

Damian

entry.title: Re: Celebrate Your SAP Academic Community Conference experience: Earn Your Exclusive Conference Badg
entry.content: Gran conferencia!!!... me voy con muchas ideas.

entry.title: Re: debugging software that connects to SAP ASE
entry.content: Start with the link here - https://support.sap.com/en/index.html

entry.title: Re: Conversion to S/4HANA: Issue with custom long texts and concatenated TDNAME including MATNR
entry.content:
Thanks, @RaymondGiuseppi! Yeah, I saw that "text below", but as table STXH and field TDNAME doesn't fit "Customer Extensions or Customer Tables", I considered it not really applicable for our issue.

Thanks for the link to OSS Note 2216958 which I just read. I'll check tomorrow when I'm logged in to our systems again, if the mentioned check report exists and if it was executed. And if it was executed, if table STXH and our custom text IDs get a mention in the results.

entry.title: Re: Why SAP Document AI Matters More Than Most Enterprises Think
entry.content:
Interesting perspective. In several SAP transformation programs I’ve worked on, document handling was often treated as a secondary process until teams realized how much manual effort was going into invoice validation, document matching, and exception handling.

What stood out to me in the article is the point that document AI is not just about OCR or extraction accuracy. The bigger value is reducing operational bottlenecks and improving how quickly teams can process business transactions with fewer manual touchpoints.

In large enterprise environments, even small improvements in document processing can have a noticeable impact on finance, procurement, and shared service operations.

Good read and very relevant topic as more organizations move toward AI-enabled ERP processes.

entry.title: Re: Error M8783 Negative moving average price prevents posting - how to resolve
entry.content:
3755199 - Moving average price for material would be negative- Message no. M8783 - SAP for Me
refer this KBA, its for cloud but helpful

entry.title: Re: Detect SQL Anywhere development files in configure
entry.content: What do you mean by "development files"? If you mean the SDK (software development kits) for things like ODBC headers then look at SDK/Headers? If you plan to distribute source code that people need to compile and run, then they would need a licensed copy of the SQL Anywhere product to start. And then instructions from you as to how to set the INCLUDE path. Our install process creates environment variables like SQLANY17 that point to the head of the installed software. You could use this symbol as an installation-independent means for locating files.

entry.title: Re: Conversion to S/4HANA: Issue with custom long texts and concatenated TDNAME including MATNR
entry.content: The note you had included has the following text which is why I proposed a script to convert - "it may also be necessary to convert exactly this content because the conversion programs delivered by SAP usually only convert values stored by SAP standard programs."

entry.title: Re: SAP EarlyWatch Alert: UI Refresh for Improved Readability and Usability
entry.content: Great update! The refreshed EarlyWatch Alert UI looks like a meaningful step forward in making system insights easier to read and act on. Improving readability and usability is especially valuable for teams that review alerts regularly, since clearer layouts can save time and reduce the chance of missing important findings.

entry.title: Re: 🚀 AI & Claude Code Experiment (Part 1): Building an SAP Analytics Cloud Widget Add-On
entry.content:
Really interesting experiment. What I liked most was that the blog focused not only on generating code with AI, but also on the practical side of getting the widget to actually work inside SAP Analytics Cloud.

In many enterprise projects, the challenge is usually not creating a quick prototype — it’s handling integration, lifecycle behavior, data binding, and making the solution usable inside the SAC environment. The walkthrough made that part much clearer.

I also think articles like this help teams better understand where AI-assisted development can realistically speed things up and where developer experience and validation are still critical.

Good hands-on example and a very practical use case for SAC custom widgets.

entry.title: Re: Error no genera Pago Efectuado en Cuenta
entry.content: Efectivamente corriendo esa tarea se resuelve el problema, gracias por compartir la solución.

entry.title: To configure error messages for costing run
entry.content:
Need to configure below messages as "Error" message for costing run.

These messages are not available in the transaction OPR4_CK.

  1. CK461 : Routing either does not exist or contains no operations
  2. CK858 : No suitable or valid Production version found
  3. CK756 : Material in plant cannot be costed

entry.title: Re: SAP Business AI Platform: #2 What Exactly It is and Its Components
entry.content: Really helpful explanation. It makes the SAP Business AI Platform feel much clearer, especially by breaking down the components in a simple way. I also like how it shows the bigger picture of how SAP is bringing AI into business applications in a practical, unified way.

entry.title: Re: find sql anywhere development files
entry.content: You need to source the configuration file. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SQL_Anywhere/61ecb3d4d8be4baaa07cc4db0ddb5d0a/8135b1bb6ce2101499f0f55a54bc1ab2.html

entry.title: How to make Matrix manager not Mandadory on the position
entry.content: Matrix manager field on position is mandadory and not all our positions should be mandadory, how do we remove that

entry.title: Re: yaai - ABAP AI tools - Evolving Toward Agentic AI
entry.content:
Hi @peter_walcz79 ,

 
The idea is to have an agent with clear instructions and access to all the tools and documentation it needs, so it can handle tasks on its own without extra user input. Some exceptions would be things like errors or human-in-the-loop steps, like asking for user approval before running a specific task.
 
Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Sap ehs
entry.content: Hi @ C.B Thank you for your reply, I will go through the link and invest my time on learning SAP EHS more deeper

entry.title: SAP Field Service and Asset Management: The Next Evolution of SAP Field Service Management
entry.content:
At SAP Sapphire Orlando, we introduced SAP Field Service and Asset Management (FSA) – the next major evolution of SAP Field Service Management.

After a decade working in the asset and service space at SAP, this launch feels like one of the most important steps forward how asset and service work gets done. 

SAP Field Service and Asset Management builds on the proven SAP Field Service Management foundation and extends it with enhanced asset management capabilities, deeper SAP Cloud ERP integration, mobile-first execution, and AI-driven automation.

It is not a separate product. It is a strategic evolution of SAP Field Service Management – bringing asset operations, field service execution, and enterprise context together in one connected execution layer.

Official launch at SAP Sapphire Orlando 2026. Pictured left to right: Viktor Kehayov (SAP), Melissa Hogan (EDF Power Solutions), Ryan Jones (SAP).


Why this matters

Asset-intensive, product-centric, and service-driven organizations are facing a new level of operational complexity. Assets are more connected. Workforces are more distributed. Service and maintenance demand is more dynamic. And the challenge is no longer just managing work orders or service calls.

The bigger question is this: can organizations connect work to asset management, customer, workforce capacity, supply chain readiness, cost impact, and business priorities in real time? And is the underlying data consistent and contextual enough to support AI automation?

EAM brings the depth of asset and maintenance processes. FSM brings the agility of execution – scheduling, dispatching, routing, mobile work, and remote workforce optimization. Many organizations now need both. And they need both connected to the business context of SAP Cloud ERP.

That is the opportunity SAP Field Service and Asset Management addresses.

One connected execution layer

SAP Field Service and Asset Management brings together the SAP Field Service Management foundation, enhanced asset management capabilities, and SAP Cloud ERP context into one execution layer for service and asset operations.

With FSA, organizations can better orchestrate the end-to-end flow of work – from planning and scheduling through dispatch, mobile execution, and analysis – in a mobile-first environment supported by AI agents and intelligent workflows.

SAP Field Service and Asset Management brings together the FSM foundation, asset management capabilities, and SAP Cloud ERP context into one unified execution layer.See it in action

In the demo, AI agents act as intelligent copilots for service and maintenance execution. Instead of starting from a blank screen, the user is guided to what needs attention, why it matters, and what action can be taken next.

Service and maintenance demand can be evaluated against available workforce capacity, risk, asset criticality, and job criticality. From there, a dispatcher agent can recommend an optimized schedule and dispatch plan through Joule before the work is executed by a technician through a modern mobile experience.

What matters is that the process stays connected.  Asset context, parts availability, business priorities, and execution updates are not sitting in separate places. They are part of one flow – helping planning, supply chain, finance, and field execution stay synchronized.


From recommendation to execution

The real value of AI in service and maintenance is not only the recommendation, but what happens next. An agent can identify an issue, explain the context, recommend an action, and help move that action into planning, scheduling, dispatch, and execution. That is where the value becomes tangible. The user remains in control. The agent helps reduce manual effort, speed up decision-making, and move work forward with more context and confidence.

This is also an important part of SAP’s vision for the Autonomous Enterprise. Not AI as a disconnected tool, but AI that delivers real business value – where people set direction and AI executes, with governance at every step.

Closed-loop execution is central to the story. When work is completed in the field, the outcome should flow back into the enterprise – updating the asset record, informing future maintenance strategies, synchronizing cost and material impacts, and improving how future work is planned and executed.

FSA connects planning, scheduling, dispatch, mobile execution, and analytics in one AI-assisted execution layer.

What this means for existing customers

What changes with SAP Field Service and Asset Management is not only the name. It is the direction of the solution. FSA builds on the proven success of SAP Field Service Management to support broader asset-centric execution, deeper SAP Cloud ERP integration, embedded AI, and a path toward more autonomous operations.

For existing SAP Field Service Management customers, this is especially important. Customers can take advantage of new functionality within their existing cloud subscription as part of the regular release cycle, beginning with the 2605 release. That means customers can benefit from the expanded direction of the solution without treating this announcement as a disruptive product change.

FSA also gives customers a practical path to deploy fast and scale with confidence. It extends embedded capabilities in SAP Cloud ERP with modern execution experiences, industry-specific capabilities, and mobile-first operations – helping organizations move quickly without turning every improvement into a multi-year transformation program.

Looking ahead

For me, the most exciting part of this launch is the shift in execution. SAP Field Service and Asset Management helps customers move from separate operational flows to one connected execution model – where asset context, workforce capacity, supply chain, finance, and field execution come together in real time.

It also gives customers a practical starting point for AI in the flow of work. Not as a future concept, but as a way to help users identify issues earlier, evaluate options faster, act with intelligence, and move to increasingly autonomous operations. 

That is the promise of SAP Field Service and Asset Management: a unified, AI-powered execution layer connected to SAP Cloud ERP and built for the next era of service and asset operations.

To learn more about SAP Field Service and Asset Management, visit our website.

entry.title: ‌🇸🇰‌Webinar: Mandatory electronic invoicing in Slovakia with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
Is your organisation ready to address Slovakia’s upcoming mandatory e‑invoicing requirements?

Slovakia is introducing mandatory structured electronic invoicing for both business-to-business (B2B) and business-to-government (B2G) transactions. Effective 1 January 2027, all VAT-registered businesses will be required to comply with a Peppol-based 5-corner model, marking a significant shift in invoicing processes and compliance obligations.

This session is intended to help organisations understand the upcoming mandate and assess its impact on existing processes and systems. It will provide a clear and structured view of what is changing and how SAP solutions can support the transition.

Speakers:
Erika Buson, Product Management SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, SAP
Marek Seversky, Localization Product Manager for SAP Cloud ERP, SAP
Martin Schmidt, Chief Architect SAP, SAP

REGISTRATION LINK

entry.title: Re: Multistage advanced intercompany sales and stock transfer with S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release
entry.content:
Hi,
has any client implemented multi-stage scenario

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Employee central exam- New format
entry.content: Need system practice more.. Focus and practice scenarios provided by SAP in “Prepare Exam”.. This is very useful for exam..

entry.title: Re: Fiori List Report Issue with OData V4 VS V2
entry.content: So Venkat, it appears you are confirming that the fact that the 'GO' button works without filling out those adopted fields proves exactly what we suspected per the above wherein the backend is not enforcing it and it is purely a visual rendering glitch on the UI layer. Because the mandatory parameters force the Odata V4 framework to wrap the metadata in an execution path container, the UI may be accidentally leaks that mandatory status styling * (Mandatory) down to the other standard fields.

entry.title: Re: Allocating freight costs to multiple charges in the billing document, not just HD00
entry.content: Any updates on the below, iam also having the same issue

entry.title: Re: Multistage advanced intercompany sales and stock transfer with S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release
entry.content: is there a way that we can practice this flow in SAP Learning hub practice system

entry.title: Re: From Intelligent to Autonomous: What Actually Changed at SAP Sapphire 2026
entry.content:
Good perspective on the shift from “intelligent” systems to more autonomous enterprise processes. One thing I’ve noticed in large SAP programs is that most organizations are still comfortable with AI recommendations, but they become much more cautious when systems start making operational decisions automatically.

That’s why the governance aspect becomes so important. In ERP environments, even a small incorrect decision can create downstream impact across finance, procurement, supply chain, or compliance processes. The real challenge is not just adding AI capabilities — it’s making sure the business context, controls, and accountability are still clear.

I also think Sapphire 2026 showed that SAP is trying to move AI beyond isolated copilots and into day-to-day enterprise operations. It will be interesting to see how quickly customers adopt autonomous workflows in production environments, especially in heavily regulated industries.

Really enjoyed the balanced take in the article.

entry.title: Re: Custom CSS is now missing in SAC stories???
entry.content:
@jbarlowjb 

James,

I can still see the css on the toolbar and ribbon and also in the styling panel.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Planning your transition paths to SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content: Very useful post. The transition paths are explained in a practical way, and it’s helpful to see how SAP is thinking about modernization without forcing everyone into a single migration approach. The Lift, Shift, and Innovate structure makes the journey feel much more realistic for existing BW and analytics customers.

entry.title: Re: Integrating IRIS
entry.content: Excellent! Thank you.

entry.title: Re: AI Agents Are Now a Board-Level Risk. Is Your Governance Ready?
entry.content:
Very relevant topic, especially now that AI agents are starting to move beyond copilots and into operational decision-making inside enterprise systems.

In traditional SAP environments, governance was mostly focused on users, roles, approvals, and process controls. But autonomous agents introduce a completely different challenge because they can potentially trigger actions across finance, procurement, HR, or supply chain processes at machine speed. That changes the conversation from simple automation to accountability and risk management.

What stood out to me is the idea that AI governance is becoming a board-level discussion rather than only a technical or security concern. Once AI agents start interacting with sensitive business processes, organizations will need clear visibility into decision logic, auditability, access controls, and escalation paths. Otherwise even small errors can create significant downstream operational or compliance impact. 

I also think many enterprises are still underestimating how much governance maturity will be required before autonomous workflows can scale safely in production environments. Interesting article and definitely an important discussion area going forward.

entry.title: Re: Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Certified – SAP Business Data Cloud Fast-Track Session
entry.content: Great initiative. This kind of learning accelerator is really valuable because it helps partners get practical, hands-on understanding of SAP Business Data Cloud instead of just theoretical knowledge. I especially like that it supports faster adoption by making the certification path feel more accessible and structured.

entry.title: Join tomorrow's live session on SAP BDC Product Generator
entry.content:
Join us on May 22nd at 9:30 - 10:30 CEST (= central Europeas summer time) for a new session regarding the popular topic of 

"Modernize SAP BW with SAP Business Data Cloud"

We will show you which options you have to preserve the investment of your legacy BW data warehouse and yet move on towards a more modern SAP Business Data Cloud centered data mesh approach. Understand how the data product generator tool supports your transition and why you should be open for a new mind set regarding data management and modeling.

Like in all live sessions, you can ask questions. 

Here you find the link to register: 

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/generating-data-products-from-sap-bw-and-sap-bw-4hana-in-sap-business-data-cloud?searchId=66a0d739-b710-4629-a29d-0e6f25ca6737&listPosition=1 

Next dates after May 22 are July 14 and Aug 5.

More live sessions can be found here: https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions 

See you soon. 

Alexander

entry.title: Recommended way to retrieve an OTR long text by alias in ABAP
entry.content:
Hi,
with transactions SOTR_EDIT and SE63 it is possible to provide texts and translations. These texts can be loaded in ABAP code using function modules. When creating a new entry in SOTR_EDIT you have to choose between short and long text. When retrieving the texts in ABAP everything is working fine with short texts - but not with long texts.
 
Method CL_WD_UTILITIES=>GET_OTR_TEXT_BY_ALIAS works for both short and long texts. But the description of the method contains a warning ("DO NOT USE").
 
Function module SOTR_GET_TEXT_KEY can only retrieve short texts.
 
Function module SOTR_STRING_READ_TEXT_WITH_KEY could read long text, but you have to provide the concept id.
The function module SOTR_GET_KEY_FOR_ALIAS can retrieve concept ids for short text, but not for long text.
 
So I'm stuck here and I'd  like to know what is the recommended way to retrieve an OTR long text for an alias in ABAP? Which methods or function modules do you use?
 
Kind regards
Guido

entry.title: Re: Modernize SAP PI/PO Landscapes with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Good topic, especially because many organizations are now reaching the point where maintaining older PI/PO landscapes is becoming more difficult than modernizing them.

In several SAP programs I’ve seen, integration complexity tends to grow quietly over the years — multiple interfaces, custom mappings, monitoring gaps, and aging middleware dependencies eventually become operational challenges during S/4HANA transformations or cloud adoption initiatives.

What I liked about the article is that it focuses not only on technical migration, but also on simplifying integration architecture and improving long-term maintainability. Moving toward Integration Suite feels less like a pure platform upgrade and more like an opportunity to modernize how integrations are monitored, governed, and scaled across hybrid environments.

The transition path is still a major concern for many customers though, especially for organizations with large PI/PO footprints and heavily customized interfaces. Practical migration guidance like this is definitely useful.

entry.title: Re: SFPLU-10849 | Manage Super Admin Access Creates Learn...
entry.content: Hi @Sunny, A super administrator is a privileged user responsible for setting up the instance or inviting others to do so. The first user created after instance provisioning is automatically granted super administrator rights.  Based on this definition, super administrators will be granted permissions accordingly. We adhere to a minimal privilege policy while ensuring a smooth customer onboarding experience, reducing manual steps and accommodating users with limited SuccessFactors knowledge.  The Admin Overseer, formerly the RBP Super Admin, is a restricted admin role dedicated to admin user management, with no operational responsibilities, and access to Manage RBP Admin Access and Manage Super Admin Access only after instance is setup.   If you would like to separate bizx and learning admins, I would suggest taking Admin Overseer type or follow RBP role configuration to authorize normal admin users. Best regards, Fannie Guan

entry.title: Re: UI Composition with Application Frontend Service
entry.content: Nice explanation. The examples make the idea of UI composition much easier to grasp, and it’s helpful to see how the Application Frontend Service can support reusable and more modular UI design. I especially like that the post connects the concept to practical use cases instead of keeping it too abstract.

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP S/4HANA Odata Service Directly with SAP APIM with Oauth2.0 Based Authentication
entry.content:
Very practical topic. A lot of organizations still have critical integrations running through PI/PO or custom middleware, so securely exposing S/4HANA OData services through APIM is becoming increasingly important during modernization programs.

What I liked about the walkthrough is that it focuses on the actual integration flow and authentication handling instead of only high-level architecture. In real projects, OAuth configuration, token handling, and endpoint security are usually where teams spend most of their troubleshooting time.

I also think approaches like this help organizations modernize integrations incrementally without forcing a complete redesign of existing landscapes all at once.

Good step-by-step explanation and a useful reference for teams working on hybrid SAP integration scenarios.

entry.title: Unable to Post Asset With TT 150 with Document Type AB in Testing Environment i.e. Z02 and Z03.
entry.content: Unable to Post Asset With TT 150 with Document Type AB in Testing Environment i.e. Z02 and Z03.

entry.title: Re: Intelligent Error Handling in SAP CPI Using AI Adapter
entry.content: This is a very practical idea. Using AI to support error handling in SAP CPI can save a lot of time during troubleshooting and make integration support much smoother. I also like that the post shows a realistic approach instead of treating AI as just a buzzword

entry.title: ‌🇩🇪‌ Webinar: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance B2B e-Invoicing in Germany
entry.content:
Electronic invoicing requirements in Germany are evolving rapidly. Since January 2025, companies must be able to receive EN 16931-compliant e-invoices. From January 2027, organisations with annual turnover above €800,000 must issue structured e-invoices for domestic B2B transactions, with all companies included from January 2028.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance supports these requirements, helping organisations manage compliant e-invoicing processes within their SAP landscape.

In this webinar, SAP localization experts will provide:

  • An overview of regulatory requirements and timelines
  • Key capabilities of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • Insights into inbound and outbound e-invoicing processes
  • A live system demonstration

Speakers:
Chi Wan Yuen, Product Manager SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, SAP SE
Sar'a Güzelkaya, Localization Product Manager Germany, SAP SE

This session will be help in German.

Join to stay informed and prepare for upcoming compliance obligations! REGISTER NOW

Curious about how SAP handles SAP Document Reporting Compliance in Germany? Join the Germany - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Work Zone group and hear directly from the experts.

entry.title: Re: debugging software that connects to SAP ASE
entry.content:
@ChrisBaker,

And then I should click "Get Support", right?

Do I need a paid account to file an issue?

 

Thank you.

 

P.S.: I think that when I interrupt the debugging session some resources are not cleaned up properly and so the server thinks there is still a connection.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG + Azure AD - Part 3: A Unified View - Azure Data Inside MDG UI
entry.content: Great post — integrating Azure AD data directly into the MDG UI solves a very real challenge for organizations that manage identity attributes across SAP and Azure. Having this unified view during governance processes reduces manual checks and improves data quality significantly. I’m especially interested in how you handle performance, caching, and synchronization when calling Azure services from MDG. Looking forward to the next part of the series.

entry.title: Re: Detect SQL Anywhere development files in configure
entry.content:
@Jack_shueler,

1. By saying "development files"  I mean the h and so files for the C/C++ software.

2. I can't look at the SDK headers, because I have no idea where that person installs the SDK. The installer explicitly asks where to install the files.

3. Yes I understand that they will need a licensed copy. And I presume that that will be the case.

4. Why do I need to supply such instructions? All I should be able to do is:

a. In configure.ac find where the SQL Anywhere client is installed.

b. Pass this information to Makefile for CXXFLAGS and LDFLAGS.

c. Compile and run.

5. It looks like the SQLANY17 environment variable is NOT available. Hence my question.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Deploying SAP HANA 2.0 on AWS Using Terraform and Ansible
entry.content: Great walkthrough — this is a clean, practical example of automating HANA provisioning on AWS using Terraform and Ansible. I appreciate how you highlighted real‑world gotchas like disk sizing, LSS requirements, and SSH timeouts during hdblcm. These are exactly the issues that trip up many first‑time deployments. The end‑to‑end automation pattern you’ve outlined is solid and very reusable across other SAP components. Nicely done.

entry.title: Re: Text Processing in NLP
entry.content: Nice introduction to the topic. The post gives a clear view of how text processing supports NLP and why preprocessing steps matter before applying models. I especially like that it helps connect the theory with the practical pipeline used in real-world NLP work.

entry.title: Re: Important Update: Review Onboarding User Permissions
entry.content:
Hi Damian,

Thanks for your update. so you granted the persmission below right?:

  • Employee Central HRIS OData API (read-only)

Thanks, 

Sevda

entry.title: ‌🇧🇷‌ Webinar: Tax Reform - Phase II: how to address priority themes for January 2027
entry.content:
Phase II of the Tax Reform is in progress and introduces significant implications for business processes, systems, and tax operations. Early preparation is essential to ensure compliance and operational stability by January 2027.

In this webcast, EY and SAP provide a combined perspective, aligning fiscal interpretation with SAP’s solution roadmap to support a structured and secure transition.

Agenda

Phase II overview
Scope, current status, key milestones, and business impacts.

Technical clarifications (EY)
Focus on priority topics with practical guidance for planning.

SAP readiness
Overview of SAP’s planned deliverables and approach to compliance support.

Q&A session
Discussion of participant questions and specific scenarios.

This session is intended for professionals responsible for tax, finance, and SAP system operations seeking a clear and practical view of upcoming requirements.

REGISTER NOW

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP BTP DMS with SAP Cloud ALM to upload external files in SAP Cloud ALM, using Boos
entry.content:
Very useful topic, especially for organizations trying to modernize legacy integrations while still keeping governance and monitoring under control.

In many SAP programs, document handling and attachment management are often treated as secondary requirements until teams realize how much operational dependency exists around external files, approvals, audit records, and project documentation. Integrating BTP DMS with Cloud ALM feels like a practical step toward making those processes more centralized and easier to manage.

What I liked about the article is that it focuses on the actual integration flow and business use case instead of only high-level architecture. The screenshots and configuration steps make it much easier to understand how the components work together in a real scenario.

Good walkthrough and definitely relevant for teams working on SAP cloud transformation and ALM modernization initiatives.

entry.title: Re: New ways in S4HANA to enhance or add input fields in the Transportation Cockpit using Field Cata
entry.content: Great Blog !!! Thank you for sharing

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - External logging to Datadog Using OpenTelemetry
entry.content: Very interesting approach. Using OpenTelemetry for external logging with Datadog makes the integration story feel much more modern and flexible. I like that the post focuses on a practical observability setup that can actually help teams troubleshoot faster and keep better visibility across SAP Integration Suite.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Post Asset With TT 150 with Document Type A...
entry.content:
Hi Mallikarjuna,

Please check the below points in Z02/Z03:

  1. Transaction Type 150 is normally used for asset acquisition/transfer scenarios and must be allowed for the asset class/asset status. SAP confirms transaction types control how FI-AA business transactions are processed. (SAP Help Portal)

  2. Check whether the asset is complete, capitalized, and open for posting in AS03/AS02.

  3. Compare config between working client and Z02/Z03:

    • AO73 / OAYZ – depreciation area and asset class settings

    • AO90 – account determination

    • OBA7 – document type AB settings

    • OB52 – posting period open for asset/G/L

    • OAYR / company code asset settings

    • Asset class allowed transaction type group

  4. If the error is AA834 / “You cannot use this transaction type to post to this asset”, then the likely issue is that transaction type group for TT 150 is not permitted for the asset/asset class. SAP KBA preview also points to allowing the transaction type group for the asset class. (SAP Support Portal)

  5. Also verify whether this is an affiliated company/vendor trading partner scenario. In such cases, SAP Community references using TT 145 or 150 depending on the setup. (SAP Community)

Ask functional/FI-AA team to compare Z02/Z03 with a working client and transport the missing FI-AA configuration. 

 

Regards , 

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: ABAP development under the Clean Core paradigm: From BAPIs to BO and CDS
entry.content:
Excellent breakdown of how ABAP development is evolving under the clean core paradigm. Your explanation of the transition from tightly coupled BAPI-based patterns to BO-based APIs, CDS models, and RAP-aligned extensibility reflects what we’re seeing across large S/4HANA programs. The focus on API stability, semantic data modeling, and upgrade-safe extension points is critical — especially as customers modernize custom code and reduce modification debt.

It would be great to see deeper dives into topics like:

  • mapping legacy BAPI logic into behavior definitions

  • handling complex validations and determinations in RAP

  • strategies for refactoring enhancement-heavy modules into clean core-compatible services

  • governance models for managing custom APIs alongside SAP standard ones

Overall, a very timely and practical contribution for teams navigating the clean core journey.

entry.title: Re: Como integrar um serviço BTP Cloud Foundry a um ambiente S4HANA On-premise
entry.content:
Interesting integration scenario. A lot of organizations still have critical S/4HANA processes running on-premise while newer applications and services are being developed on BTP, so secure connectivity between those environments is becoming increasingly important.

What I liked about the article is that it focuses on the practical setup and communication flow instead of only discussing the architecture at a high level. In real projects, connectivity, authentication, network configuration, and trust setup are usually the areas where teams spend most of their troubleshooting time.

Hybrid landscapes will probably remain common for quite a while, especially in large enterprises with complex SAP environments, so walkthroughs like this are definitely useful for implementation teams.

entry.title: Re: Cannot delete fields from Z* incompletion procedure (...
entry.content:
For user fix, try this:

Go to CBC → Define Incompleteness Procedures.
Do not delete directly from Z2 first.
Check where Z2 is assigned:
Sales document type
Item category
Schedule line/category if applicable
If Z2 is copied from standard and fields are protected/greyed out, create a new custom incompletion procedure, for example ZZ2, instead of modifying Z2.
Copy only required fields into ZZ2.
Assign ZZ2 to the relevant item category / sales document process.
Remove assignment of old Z2 from that item/category.
Save and transport.
Retest by creating a new sales order.

entry.title: User Consumption Report for Particular Time Period
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to get a report of user login consumption for a particular time period.

Example: In a month, how many hours the user has logged in. 

How to get this report?

 

Thanks. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: User Consumption Report for Particular Time Period
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

 

The information of how many hours the users have logged in the system is not available. The system doesn't track or calculate cumulative login hours per user.

Because it is a cloud environment, sessions frequently time out, run in background threads, or stay active via single sign-on (SSO) tokens, meaning duration metrics aren't a reliable or standard KPI recorded in the backend.

There is a pre-delivered standard CDS view that captures Identity and Access Management (IAM) logon metadata.  It doesn't calculate how many hours the users logged in, but if you want a report showing who has logged in, when they last logged in, or active login instances within a period, you can use the CDS View I_IAMBusinessUserLogonDetails.

You can create a Custom CDS View using this CDS as data source. To create a Custom CDS View check documentation SAP Help Portal | Creating Custom CDS Views. 

For more information about Identity and Access Management, check KBA 3212871 - How to get "Identity and Access Management" information - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Message MM 309
entry.content:
It worked here, thanks to our colleague ian, Wong Loke Foong that mentioned:

"You can change the valuation class via MM02 -

fill in the new valuation class for the current and also for the two previous periods.

On the view Accounting1 there is a push button with the

name 'Previous period/year'. If you press this button, you see the..." valuation class empty.

So guys, please note that, for me took a while to understand it due to the languague barier 😄 but as soon as you open the accounting 1, you'll see that inside this tab there will be more tabs, search in your screen for the Period 005.2026, Period 04.2026 and period 012.2025. Then you just have to go in each of these tabs and fill the valuation class.

Hope I could add something.

João Cavalheri

entry.title: /users/current/ being appended to URLs after Composable Storefront and SAP Commerce Cloud upgrade
entry.content:
Hi Team, we have upgraded our Composable Storefront and SAP Commerce Cloud to newer versions:

  • Composable Storefront: 2211.37.0 > 221121.7

  • SAP Commerce Cloud: 2211.43 > 2211-jdk21.7

But there is an issue observed change in the OOTB cms APIs - /cms/pages and /cms/components.

BEFORE upgrade:

  • /occ/v2/campbellsretailersspa/cms/pages?PERSON_NAME=en&curr=AUD 

AFTER upgrade:

  • /occ/v2/campbellsretailersspa/users/current/cms/pages?PERSON_NAME=en&curr=AU

As a result, the APIs have started failing. So, the question is whether this additional /users/current/ part can be stripped from the API requests, or is there any other fix available in this regard?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: /users/current/ being appended to URLs after Composab...
entry.content:
Hey there!

This is a technical change introduced in the Composable Storefront version 221121.7 for the OccCmsComponentAdapter and OccCmsPageAdapter. We removed all logic related to the USER_CMS_ENDPOINTS constant and removed usage of FeatureConfigService.

For the OccCmsComponentAdapter, the adapter now always uses the userIdService to determine the current user, and builds the component request URL accordingly.

For the OccCmsPageAdapter, similar to the component adapter, it now always fetches the user ID to build CMS page request URLs.

Now all CMS component and page requests now consistently include the user context in the endpoint URL. The endpoint format is now standardized: /users/{userId}/cms/components and /users/{userId}/cms/pages is always used instead of the legacy /cms/components and /cms/pages format.

The conditional branching based on USER_CMS_ENDPOINTS constant has been eliminated, simplifying the code path. Along with ensuring personalized CMS content is consistently served, based on the authenticated or anonymous user context.

Kind Regards,
Wesley
SAP Support

entry.title: Alteração de codigo de imposto em lançamento advindo do modulo de venda futura
entry.content:
Boa tarde!!

A empresa que trabalha emite nota fiscal de venda futura e posteriormente envia nota de remessa para entrega do produto. 

Atualmente não consigo emitir uma nota de remessa vinculada a uma nota de faturamento simples ( módulo: vedas-> Venda futura) porque no momento de " copiar para - entrega" o sistema não me permite alterar o código de imposto. A nota de remessa deve fazer a contabilização descrita abaixo, pois a nota de simples fatura somente contábil Cliente (Ativo) contra a conta de simples faturamento (Passivo) e está correta devido ao formato de nossa operação. Minha pergunta é: Qual o parâmetro devo fazer para que no momento de copiar uma nota de faturamento simples (módulo: vedas-> Venda futura) o SAP B1 permita a alteração do código de imposto?

Dificuldades no processo:

1. No procedimento de copiar a nota de simples faturamento para entrega, o sistema não deixa alterar o codigo de imposto: Como liberamos o sistema para conseguirmos alterar o codigo de imposto na tela de entrega?

2.  A contabilização na Tela de entrega deve ser a seguinte: 

D - Venda para Entrega Futura (Passivo Circulante) 

C - Receita Bruta de Vendas (Resultado) 

C - Pis a recolher Passivo (Passivo) 

D – Cofins a recolher do passivo (Passivo) 

D - (-) Pis a recolher Passivo (Resultado)

C – (-) Cofins a recolher do passivo (Resultado) 

D - Custo das Mercadorias Vendidas - CMV (Resultado)

C - Estoque de Mercadorias (Ativo Circulante) 

Desde já agradeço. 

Atenciosamente

entry.title: Re: UI5 Goes Mad in Dorset!
entry.content:
Thanks for the nice write up, but I must admit that I love me some ABAP/HR LDBs.  (Object oriented Select and they are very fast, unlike customer LDBs).  As a bonus I get to use the STOP command, global variables that pi§§ off the code check and I can use END-OF-SELECTION correctly unlike 99% of my colleagues.  I know, I have a boring life.

Also, I have decided to keep using that Hungarian notation for two reasons.

1.  It is all over ABAP code from SAP and everyone for the past 25+ years and most "gets it".

2.  My Hungarian colleagues were, bar none, the best ABAPers with whom I have ever worked.

entry.title: PRINTING EWM LOCATION LABELS IN THE PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content:
Dear SAP team

The business needs to be able to print location labels.

We can see that the app is available for CLOUD PRIVATE

But in CLOUD PUBLIC, that option isn't available

What solution does SAP offer for printing EWM storage bin labels?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements ALP (OData V2): Basic Search field sho...
entry.content:
your ui5 version is really too old.

when you do local development, can u switch it to a higher version?

if the search box disappear, it means your version is just not respecting the annotation.

entry.title: Re: Como integrar um serviço BTP Cloud Foundry a um ambiente S4HANA On-premise
entry.content:
@FabioPagoti boa, gostei do seu comentário.

Sim, o contexto principal desse post seria uma aplicação CAP usando o Node com approuter, e o frontend Fiori (openui5) consumindo fontes Odata, ou seja, na prática vira um serviço que faz o roteamento e também serve arquivos estáticos.

Mas de fato esse cenário também pode ser feito de outras maneiras, como desacoplar o frontend usando o html5 repository, e também pode ser feito o deploy através do MTA.

O foco principal do post era descrever, de forma um pouco mais prática, a arquitetura da integração entre esses 3 pontos: Cloud Foundry, Cloud Connector e S4Hana, por isso não foquei tanto na parte do Node e nem do frontend.

Nas minhas pesquisas, tanto em fóruns quando usando IA, a informação sempre aparece muito fragmentada, o que dificulta o entendimento do cenário de ponta a ponta, daí surgiu a ideia desse blog post.
